description_long: This object consists of three metal open-ended wrenches, each with a distinct size, arranged upright and leaning against each other. The wrenches are made of dark, aged metal, showing signs of wear and a matte, slightly rough surface. Each wrench features two U-shaped open jaws at either end, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The handles are straight and flat, with no visible grip pattern or rubber inserts, and the metal surface appears to have some minor rust and discoloration, especially near the jaws and edges. The wrenches are utilitarian in design, lacking any visible brand markings or decorative elements. Their overall appearance suggests they are well-used, possibly found in a garage, workshop, or urban maintenance setting.
description: A group of three aged, open-ended metal wrenches with dark, worn surfaces and no visible grip pattern or branding. The wrenches are utilitarian and show signs of rust and wear.
description_view_0: Three open-ended wrenches are seen upright, with their jaws and handles clearly visible, showing the worn, dark metal surface.
description_view_1: The wrenches are viewed from an angle highlighting the thickness of the handles and the open jaws, with more shadow and less detail on the faces.
description_view_2: A nearly head-on view of the wrenches, emphasizing the alignment of the handles and the symmetry of the open jaws.
description_view_3: The wrenches are seen from the opposite side, with the curvature of the jaws and the overlapping handles more prominent.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible rust and wear marks are present, especially near the jaws and edges, with moderate severity.
